TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[✓] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[✓] HELEN   [✓] OTHER Susan

FROM: Helen via Jeanne
DATE: 8/3/82

[XX] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: Gov. Code Section 84206
INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [XX]

CALLER______________________________
REPRESENTING_______________________
PHONE NUMBER:_______________________

QUESTION: If a pledge to make a late contribution is reported by telegram within 48 hours of the time the pledge is made, is another late report required within 48 hours when the pledge is actually paid?

ANSWER: NO. If a pledge (enforceable promise to make a payment) made during the late contribution period is reported pursuant to Section 84206, so long as none of the information originally reported changes, another late report is not required to be filed within 48 hours of the time the pledge is actually paid.
